zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Harmon於1510年出生在英國倫敦時，她的父親William Harman24歲，母親Margaret Boteler25歲。她於1526年與Sir John Page Leonard, III, High Barrister at Lincoln Inn, Sheriff of Kent在英國肯特設維寧聖 波托爾福也斯 邱奇結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她去世於1585年10月26日在英國肯特設維寧享年75歲，埋葬在英國肯特設維寧聖 波托爾福也斯 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Harmon于1510年出生在英国倫敦时，她的父亲William Harman24岁，母亲Margaret Boteler25岁。她于1526年与Sir John Page Leonard, III, High Barrister at Lincoln Inn, Sheriff of Kent在英国肯特設維寧聖 波托爾福也斯 邱奇结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和7个女儿的父母。她去世于1585年10月26日在英国肯特設維寧享年75岁，埋葬在英国肯特設維寧聖 波托爾福也斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Harmon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Anglo-Norman French and Middle English personal name Herman, adopted from ancient Germanic Hariman, Her(e)man, composed of elements meaning ‘army’ + ‘man’.
Americanized form of German   Harmann   or   Hermann  . Compare   Harman  .

variants.name: Harmond
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Harmon  .

variants.name: Hargadon
meanings: Irish (Sligo): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hArgadáin ‘descendant of Argadán’, a personal name from a diminutive of argad ‘silver’. In Galway the traditional form O'Hargadan has tended to be replaced by Hardeman, and elsewhere in Ireland by Harman and Harmon .

variants.name: Harmony
meanings: Variant of   Harmon  , a surname of German origin.
English: unexplained. This surname is very rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Harmon was born in 1510, in London, England, her father, William Harman, was 24 and her mother, Margaret Boteler, was 25. She married Sir John Page Leonard, III, High Barrister at Lincoln Inn, Sheriff of Kent in 1526, in St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 7 daughters. She died on 26 October 1585, in Chevening, Kent, England, at the age of 75, and was buried in St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Harmon est née en 1510, à Londres, Angleterre, son père, William Harman, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Margaret Boteler, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John Page Leonard, III, High Barrister at Lincoln Inn, Sheriff of Kent en 1526, à St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 7 filles. Elle est décédée le 26 octobre 1585, à Chevening, Kent, Angleterre, à l’âge de 75 ans, et a été enterrée à St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, Angleterre.

Sie hat 1526 in St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, England Sir John Page Leonard, III, High Barrister at Lincoln Inn, Sheriff of Kent geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Sie starb am 26. Oktober 1585 im Alter von 75 Jahren in Chevening, Kent, England und wurde in St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Harmon è nata il nel 1510 nel London, England, sua padre, William Harman, aveva 24 anni e sua madre, Margaret Boteler, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato Sir John Page Leonard, III, High Barrister at Lincoln Inn, Sheriff of Kent nel 1526, nel St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 7 figlie. Morì il 26 ottobre 1585, nel Chevening, Kent, England, all'età di 75 anni, e fu sepolta a St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Harmonが1510年にイギリスロンドンで生まれた時，父William Harmanは24歳，母Margaret Botelerは25歳であった。1526年にイギリスケントチェベニングスト ボトルフ'ス チャーチでSir John Page Leonard, III, High Barrister at Lincoln Inn, Sheriff of Kentと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼女は1585年10月26日にイギリスケントチェベニングで75歳で死去し，イギリスケントチェベニングスト ボトルフ'ス チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, En: 영국 켄트 처베닝 스트 벗프'스 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Harmon(이)가 1510년 영국 런던에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Harman(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Boteler(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1526년 영국 켄트 처베닝 스트 벗프'스 c후르치에서 Sir John Page Leonard, III, High Barrister at Lincoln Inn, Sheriff of Kent(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1585년 10월 26일 영국 켄트 처베닝에서 75세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 켄트 처베닝 스트 벗프'스 c후르치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Harmon nasceu em 1510, em London, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Harman, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Boteler, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir John Page Leonard, III, High Barrister at Lincoln Inn, Sheriff of Kent em 1526, em St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 7 filhas. Ela faleceu em 26 de outubro de 1585, em Chevening, Kent, Inglaterra, com 75 anos, e foi sepultada em St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, Inglaterra.

ru.St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, En: ст ботолф'с чурч, чевенинг, кэнт, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Harmon родилась в 1510 г. в местности Лондон, Англия, её отцу по имени William Harman было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret Boteler, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Page Leonard, III, High Barrister at Lincoln Inn, Sheriff of Kent в 1526 г. в местности ст ботолф'с чурч, чевенинг, кэнт, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Она умерла 26 октября 1585 г. в местности чевенинг, кэнт, Англия в возрасте 75 лет и была похоронена в местности ст ботолф'с чурч, чевенинг, кэнт, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Harmon nació en 1510, en London, Inglaterra, su papá, William Harman, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Margaret Boteler, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Sir John Page Leonard, III, High Barrister at Lincoln Inn, Sheriff of Kent en 1526, en St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió el 26 de octubre de 1585, en Chevening, Kent, Inglaterra, a la edad de 75 años, y fue sepultada en St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, Inglaterra.
